rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNI ZOCCA VS. ANNA MARIA ZOCCA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 21, PLAINTIFF GIOVANNI ZOCCA'S Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal; Request For Judicial Notice. Grant. As the Court of Appeal opinion earlier filed in this case notes, the matter concerns a twenty-five year old inter-family dispute. What apparently started as a $25,000 case (reduced by set-off to nothing) has survived over the last years just as prolonged dispute over attorneys fees-- fees which now far, far outweigh the worth of the underlying claims. Now, as before, defendants' actions have unnecessarily increased the costs to plaintiff, not to speak of to defendants themselves. Here, defendants present no evidence that the billed time was excessive, and they note no entries or tasks for which the time ought not to have spent. There is no evidence that the claimed rate is excessive, and the legal argument on the unavailability of fees is not persuasive: The appeal for which fees are sought involved entitlement to fees, which entitlement justifies the award now. The request for judicial notice is unopposed and granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MEREDITH I RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Motion For Order Requiring Pltff To Furnish Security As A Vexatious Litigant Under Ccp 369.1; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANTS WENDY WONG, MASON MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC FKA PRUDENTIAL'S Joint Motion For Order Requiring PlAINTIff To Furnish Security As A Vexatious Litigant Under Ccp 369.1. Granted. Mason McDuffie Real Estate Inc. fka Prudential California Realty and erroneously sued as Prudential Realty of California, Wendy Wong, Meredith Richardson, Lauren Richardson, Christopher Miller and Holley Miller have made a showing that Mr. Eng is a vexatious litigant and that there is not a reasonable probability that Mr. Eng will prevail in the litigation against these defendants. Eng has repeatedly been found to be a vexatious litigant, such as in the November 9, 2010 order in case No. 10-496656, and other mentioned in the moving papers. Eng must file a bond or security in the amount of $44,000 not later than August 8, 2012 or else the complaint will be dismissed as to Mason McDuffie Real Estate Inc. fka Prudential California Realty erroneously sued as Prudential Realty of California, and Wendy Wong. Eng must file a bond or security in the amount of $44,000 not later than August 8, 2012 or else the complaint will be dismissed as to Meredith Richardson, Lauren Richardson, Christopher Miller and Holley Miller. The intent of this order is to ensure that for all defendants security in the total sum of $88,000 is posted. The Request for Judicial Notice is unopposed and granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RAMA MODY VS. IGOR KRYAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Terminating Sanctions; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF RAMA MODY'S MOTION FOR Terminating Sanctions AND REQUEST For Judicial Notice. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Woodson as follows and as modified: Denied. Monetary sanctions cannot be granted because there is no declaration in support of such sanctions. No specific authority is presented for issuing terminating sanctions, and the moving papers are unclear whether terminating sanctions are sought for (1) violations of prior orders or (2) generally for assertedly false statements in filings, and in the later case, no authority is provided for the relief sought. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: SF MARINA HARBOR ASSOCIATION A NOT-FOR-PROFIT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A POLITICAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Prelim Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 10, PETITIONERS SF MARINA HARBOR ASSOCIATION A NOT-FOR-PROFIT, BRUCE MUNRO, BRIGHT WINN, PAUL MANNING, ALAN CAVEY, GRACE KNIGHT, JOSEPH ANDRESEN'S Motion For An Order To Show Cause ReGARDING PrelimINARY Injunction. City's Request for Judicial Notice: Granted as unopposed. Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Prelim Injunction: Denied. Petitioners are not likely to prevail on the merits, and have not shown irreparable harm. While courts review both these factors, allowing for perhaps less evidence on one if there is very strong evidence on the other, some probability of success on the merits is nevertheless essential. In re Quoc Thai Pham, 195 CA4th 681, 685 (2011). On the contract and implied covenant claims, it appears that defendants were authorized to approve New Rules and that petitioners-owners previously agreed and understood new rules (subsequent amendments) could be unilaterally imposed. No good demonstration of a fiduciary duty has been made out, at least not one that shows the petitioners, rather than the public, is due such duties, for any trust beneficiaries would be the public. (Petitioners do not seem to disagree, Reply at 9 line 6, but thus it is difficult to know why Petitioners should be entitled to something that other California citizens do not have- i.e. berthing rights.) The violation of due process claims presumes an entitlement to property which in turn probably depends on the strength of the breach of contract claims, suggesting the due process claim is weak. Further, that claim assumes that the petitioners had such a perpetual interest in the berths that they could literally transfer these to heirs (Reply at 10), an interest which, at this stage of the case, appears improbable. As far as procedural due process is concerned, some such process was afforded, and it does not appear likely that this was so deficient that the defendants' proposed new rules would be unlawful. Turning our attention to irreparable harm, there are two points to be made. An animating cause of the suit seems be the complaint that petitioners spent money on renovations with an expectation of future tenancy- if there was such an unwritten understanding, and it were enforceable, the return of money appears reasonably compensatory. Secondly, Petitioners claim that they are about to be in effect evicted: The evidence does not demonstrate that is currently threatened. For all these reasons, the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JANE GELFAND VS. POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Prc'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Set Two, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Prc'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Set Two, And For Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Within 20 days of entry of this order, Plaintiff shall serve code-compliant supplemented responses to the following requests for production: Requests Nos. 1 through 13, 19 through 33, 39, 40, 44 through 64 of Plaintiff's Requests for Production - Set One; Requests Nos. 3, 4, 6, 7, 18, 22 through 25, 35, 36, 38 through 40, 42, 45, 48 & 50 of Plaintiff's Requests for Production - Set Two; Requests Nos. 1, 3, and 4 of Plaintiff's Requests for Production - Set Four. Defendant's motion is DENIED with respect to Defendant's Third Set of Requests for Production. Requests Nos. 1 through 282 of Defendant's Third Set are overly broad, unduly burdensome, and collectively verge on giving the appearance of having been propounded with the intention of harassing Plaintiff in that they request ALL documents related to Plaintiff's "dealings with or relationship to" 282 separate individuals. Document requests 283 through 286, as drafted, call for all communications mentioning four family members and are overbroad and unduly burdensome. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ESR1 INVESTMENTS LLC, et al VS. JOSEPH TIERNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Mtn For Order Compelling Contractural Arbitration And Staying Judicial Proceedings And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT JOSEPH TIERNEY (Amended) mOTION For Order Compelling Contractural Arbitration And Staying Judicial Proceedings. Denied. The contractual arbitration clause in the Agreement to Purchase and Sell Property for a 50% interest in the right, title and interest in property defined as 1250 Missouri Street, San Francisco, California does not comply with CCP § 1298(c). The Agreement is expressly one for the conveyance of real property, and accordingly § 1298 applies. Defendant does not contest that the arbitration agreement here does not comply with § 1298. Judge Karnow discloses that in excess of eight years ago on approximately two occasions he was retained and his law firm was paid by Attorney William Farrer, and perhaps by Mr Farrer's opposing counsel, to mediate cases. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CACH LLC VS. JOHNDEL P. GUBATINA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Cach Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 25, 2012, Line 4, Plaintiff Cach Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion that matters in Plaintiff's request for admissions - set one be deemed admitted is DENIED. Defendant served verified responses that substantially comply with CCP Section 2033.220 prior to the hearing, and thus CCP Section 2033.280(c) requires the motion to be denied. Section 2033.280(c) nevertheless makes sanctions mandatory since Defendant's failure to answer the requests for admissions necessitated the motion. The court finds that Plaintiff's counsel's hourly rate of $225.00 is reasonable and that the 1.5 hours Plaintiff's counsel spent in preparing the motion is also reasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt
